scenario: Aurora Bank runs 12 Spring Boot job workers that authenticate against Camunda 8 SaaS. The team upgrades to the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot starter and finds that several deprecated zeebe.client.cloud.* properties must be renamed to keep the existing OAuth client credentials working for 24000 jobs per day.
question: Which mapping correctly reflects the documented replacement for the deprecated zeebe.client.cloud.client-id property?

options.text: Rename zeebe.client.cloud.client-id to camunda.client.auth.client-id, which the Spring Boot starter properties reference page documents as the replacement on the cloud table
Rename zeebe.client.cloud.client-id to camunda.client.cloud.client-id, keeping the cloud namespace because Spring Boot binds the legacy property name through configuration aliases
Rename zeebe.client.cloud.client-id to camunda.client.identity.client-id under the identity component group so the OAuth client is shared with Operate and Tasklist by default
Rename zeebe.client.cloud.client-id to camunda.client.security.client-id under the security namespace alongside the certificate and override authority properties

a.text: Correct. The properties reference page lists camunda.client.auth.client-id (env CAMUNDA_CLIENT_AUTH_CLIENTID) as the replacement for the deprecated zeebe.client.cloud.client-id property in the zeebe.client.cloud table.

b.text: Incorrect. The replacement moves the property out of the cloud namespace into camunda.client.auth; the cloud namespace keeps only properties like cluster-id and region.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no camunda.client.identity.client-id property in the reference; OAuth client configuration moved under camunda.client.auth, not under an identity namespace.

d.text: Incorrect. The security namespace covers certificate path and override authority, not client credentials; client-id moves to camunda.client.auth.client-id.

explanation: On the Spring Boot starter properties reference, the deprecated zeebe.client.cloud table maps the legacy zeebe.client.cloud.client-id to camunda.client.auth.client-id. The cloud namespace retains only cluster-id, region, and domain replacements; OAuth credentials live under camunda.client.auth.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
